methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $235M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Electra.aero is a Series B transportation-technology company that has raised $235M to date. The broader transportation sector comp universe includes seven private peers—among them Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Arc Boats, and The ePlane Company—with no public comparables identified. The narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage matches to support a reliable peer-median valuation method.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis serves as the primary triangulation method. Using total capital raised ($235M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $446M. This method assumes Electra.aero's capital efficiency and risk profile align with median Series B progression patterns in transportation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $380M (low) to $513M (high), centered on a median of $446M. This range reflects a 1.6x to 2.2x multiple applied to raised capital. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular same-stage peer data, which limits confidence in the stage-progression multiplier, and the nascent state of the electric aviation subsector, which may not follow established transportation venture patterns. A richer dataset of Series B or Series C comps in advanced mobility would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
